sources.notes: Statement and hack format: same hidden min-max function task as H1, with n <= 200 and larger total n.
Used to confirm the H2 optimization: binary-search style probes for the top behavior and two-ended split search.
Chinese solution consulted to cross-check the shared model and recursive split-finding explanation.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution index; the article listed in sources was the actually consulted Luogu source.

annotation.summary: Reconstruct the same hidden ordered min-max function as H1, but with a larger n that requires more efficient top-operation and split discovery.
annotation.constraints: Medium version: n <= 200, sum n <= 200, and the learning-query budget remains generous enough for O(n log n).
annotation.core_idea: Keep the recursive tree reconstruction from H1, but reduce the cost of identifying the top behavior and split. Binary-search style prefix/suffix 0-1 probes locate the selected boundary in logarithmic queries, then a two-ended scan finds a valid split without paying a full quadratic cost at every recursion level. Recurse with neutral defaults outside the active interval.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) oracle queries in the intended H2 approach; final evaluations are O(n).
tricks: binary search on prefix/suffix query patterns
two-ended scan for the split position

solution_variants.name: binary-search-divide-and-conquer-reconstruction
solution_variants.summary: Use binary-search probes to determine top behavior, scan from both ends to find the split, and recursively reconstruct the ordered min-max tree.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) oracle queries, O(n) per final evaluation query

tags.evidence: The solution is an adaptive query scheme for reconstructing an unknown min-max expression tree.
tags.description: Interactive or query-based problems where the main goal is to reconstruct an unknown hidden structure or function from adaptive oracle answers.
tags.created_reason: The broad interactive tag does not distinguish reusable hidden-structure reconstruction from simple interactive search or games.

tags.evidence: The reconstruction repeatedly splits the ordered variable interval into child subexpressions and recurses.
The medium optimization explicitly locates the top selected boundary with logarithmic prefix/suffix query searches.
The split-finding phase scans candidate split positions from both ends after the top operation is known.
The recovered min-max function is represented and evaluated as an ordered expression tree.
